hyperedges.label: Extension Security Pipeline: Scanner + Zero-Trust + Worker Isolation
Shell Rendering Architecture: ShellView + Omnibar + CommandPalette + CustomEvents
Cross-Cutting Refactor Concerns: stateRef Pattern + IPC Client Pattern + Single-File Constraint
Security Isolation Triad: Worker Threads + Chroot Storage + Context Bridge
AI Orchestrator Fallback Chain: ai-orchestrator -> Ollama chatbot
Extension Packaging and Install Pipeline: nxt -> .nuxyext -> store security chain
Extension Security Isolation: Worker Thread + CoreContext Proxy + Capability Enforcement
Extension Type Role System: Tool + Provider + Orchestrator
Three-Layer Error Boundary: Kernel Broker + Frontend Resilience + Custom Element Isolation
Lit Remake: Three-Paradigm Codebase Problem (Vanilla CE + Partial Lit + Real Lit)
Security-Critical Issues Requiring Fix: Path Traversal, eval(), innerHTML XSS
IPC/Worker Timeout & Memory Leak Cluster (No Timeout → Zombie Workers)
Security Enforcement Triad: Worker Isolation, Storage Chroot, Capabilities Lattice
Full IPC Pipeline: ext:invoke → host:call/reply → IpcResponse
Extension Loading: Scanner + Worker + CoreContext Proxy
LitElement Extension Authoring Contract (createRenderRoot + @nuxy/core imports + light DOM)
Extension Backend Core API Sandboxing (fs/storage/db/shell instead of Node.js)
Keyboard Shortcut System (useToolKeyActions + activeOn + nuxy-key-hints-changed)

{

nodes.label: Worker Thread Isolation for Extension Backends
nodes.rationale: Each extension backend runs in its own Worker thread for security and isolation. Calls to core.* send host:call messages to the main thread via parentPort; replies come back as host:reply.
nodes.norm_label: worker thread isolation for extension backends

nodes.label: Two-Layer UI Component System (@nuxy/ui stubs + ui-default runtime)
nodes.rationale: packages/ui provides compile-time stubs delegating to window.UI at runtime; extensions/ui-default provides real custom element implementations. Swapping ui-default changes the entire UI without touching any consumer code.
nodes.norm_label: two-layer ui component system (@nuxy/ui stubs + ui-default runtime)

nodes.label: Useless Core Principle (Kernel = Extension Loading + IPC + Window Mgmt only)
nodes.rationale: The Core is strictly a Kernel. Its ONLY jobs are Extension Loading, Sandboxed Execution, IPC Routing, and Window Management. If it can be an extension, it MUST be an extension. This is a Gnome-Shell-style architecture.
nodes.norm_label: useless core principle (kernel = extension loading + ipc + window mgmt only)

nodes.label: Zero-Trust Default Deny Extension Permissions
nodes.rationale: Host privileges require permissions in manifest.json. Extensions CANNOT communicate directly — cross-extension calls use core.extensions.invoke via broker.ts.

nodes.label: gradient.ts — Vendored Stripe WebGL Gradient (Do Not Refactor)
nodes.rationale: gradient.ts is a verbatim copy of Stripe WebGL gradient by Kevin Hufnagl, with embedded Ashima Arts snoise (MIT) and glsl-blend (MIT). It is @ts-nocheck, minifier-artifact styled, and must be treated as an opaque black box. Any customization must happen exclusively in frontend.tsx using the Gradient public API.
nodes.norm_label: gradient.ts — vendored stripe webgl gradient (do not refactor)

nodes.label: ShellView God Component (933-line monolith with memory leak)
nodes.norm_label: shellview god component (933-line monolith with memory leak)

nodes.label: TDD Mandatory Development Workflow
nodes.rationale: TDD is mandatory for all new features. Write tests first (must fail), then minimum implementation, then iterate until full suite passes. A feature is not done until all tests are green.

nodes.label: stateRef Snapshot Pattern for Stable Keyboard Callbacks
nodes.rationale: Calendar, video-downloader, and settings all use a manually-maintained stateRef.current snapshot to feed stable keyboard callbacks, avoiding stale closures with useToolKeyActions. A shared useStableRef(state) hook is proposed.
nodes.norm_label: stateref snapshot pattern for stable keyboard callbacks

nodes.label: Duplicated ipcCall/invoke Pattern Across Extension Frontends
nodes.rationale: All 4 extension frontends (calendar, notes, clipboard, video-downloader) define their own local IPC helper with the same shape. Recommendation: provide a createIpcClient(extId) factory in @nuxy/extension-sdk to unify error handling.
nodes.norm_label: duplicated ipccall/invoke pattern across extension frontends

nodes.norm_label: theme extension system (json css custom properties)

nodes.norm_label: window.core renderer api (ipc, window, events, themes, icons)

nodes.label: LitElement Standard for Extension UI Components
nodes.rationale: LitElement with html template literals is the standard framework for UI components in extensions. The legacy h() helper from extensions/ce-utils.ts is deprecated and scheduled for removal.
nodes.norm_label: litelement standard for extension ui components

nodes.label: Vite Workspace Alias Resolution (no build step for packages)
nodes.rationale: Workspace aliases are resolved at build time in src/vite.config.ts — package source files are pointed to directly, so no build step is needed for packages during development.
nodes.norm_label: vite workspace alias resolution (no build step for packages)

nodes.label: extensions/ui-default/frontend.js Build Artifact (gitignored)
nodes.rationale: The ui-default frontend.js is a build artifact that must be regenerated with pnpm -C extensions/ui-default build, or automatically via pnpm dev. It is gitignored and not checked into the repo.

nodes.label: Extension Single Self-Contained File Constraint (no relative imports)
nodes.rationale: EXTENSION_GUIDE.md §5 mandates: frontend.tsx must be a single self-contained file — no relative imports. Sub-components, custom hooks, and utility functions may not be extracted to sibling files. All extracted logic must stay inside frontend.tsx.
nodes.norm_label: extension single self-contained file constraint (no relative imports)

hyperedges.label: Extension Security Pipeline: Scanner + Zero-Trust + Worker Isolation
Shell Rendering Architecture: ShellView + Omnibar + CommandPalette + CustomEvents
Cross-Cutting Refactor Concerns: stateRef Pattern + IPC Client Pattern + Single-File Constraint
Security Isolation Triad: Worker Threads + Chroot Storage + Context Bridge
AI Orchestrator Fallback Chain: ai-orchestrator -> Ollama chatbot
Extension Packaging and Install Pipeline: nxt -> .nuxyext -> store security chain
